description: A Japanese ink painting on a vertical scroll showing a large frontal face of Bodhidharma (Daruma), the legendary founder of Zen Buddhism. The face is rendered with bold, expressive brushstrokes — heavy brows, wide staring eyes, a broad nose, and a mustache. The figure's robes are indicated by a few simple curved lines at the bottom. Japanese calligraphic text fills the upper right portion and the artist's seal appears at the lower left. The style is characteristically Zen — spontaneous, minimal, and energetic.
